device_name: Vista OS, Vista AI Scan, RTHawk
intended_use: Vista OS is an accessory to 1.5T and 3.0T whole-body magnetic resonance diagnostic devices (MRDD). It is intended to operate alongside, and in parallel with, the existing MR console to acquire traditi
intended_use_full: Vista OS is an accessory to 1.5T and 3.0T whole-body magnetic resonance diagnostic devices (MRDD). It is intended to operate alongside, and in parallel with, the existing MR console to acquire traditional, real-time and accelerated images. Vista OS software controls the MR scanner to acquire, reconstruct and display static and dynamic transverse, coronal, sagittal, and oblique cross-sectional images that display the internal structures and/or functions of the entire body.
target_condition: cardiac imaging quality assessment; motion artifacts in MRI
ground_truth: Board-certified cardiologists and radiologists (blinded to patient information) provided reference assessments
other_metrics: Motion artifact detection: 81% agreement with cardiologist reader; Ungatable waveform detection: 80% agreement criterion met; Cardiac denoising: diagnostic quality superior to non-denoised in >80% of cases; Brain localizer: mean plane angulation error <3 degrees (SD <5), mean plane position error <5 mm (SD <15); Prostate localizer: mean 3D Intersection-over-Union ≥0.65; VENC prediction: average velocity error <10% for all vessels/views
